expectations.text: Uses SerialTypedEventBus from @ahoo-wang/fetcher-eventbus
Has ordered handlers with 'order' property (first logs action, second updates analytics)
expectations.evidence: Logger handler has order: 1, Analytics handler has order: 2

expectations.text: Demonstrates on() method to register handlers
expectations.evidence: Code shows: eventBus.on({ name: 'logger', order: 1, handle: ... })

expectations.text: Demonstrates emit() method to trigger events
Demonstrates off() method to remove handlers
Includes TypeScript type definitions for events
expectations.evidence: Code defines BaseEvent, LoginEvent, LogoutEvent, AuthEvent interfaces

expectations.text: Uses BroadcastChannelMessenger from @ahoo-wang/fetcher-eventbus
Demonstrates creating a BroadcastChannelMessenger instance
Shows postMessage or broadcast to send events to other tabs
Shows onmessage handler to receive events from other tabs
Demonstrates close() method to clean up
Uses ParallelTypedEventBus from @ahoo-wang/fetcher-eventbus
Has multiple handlers that execute concurrently (in parallel)
expectations.evidence: All three handlers (ui-updater, storage-persister, notification-sender) registered and emit uses await on Promise.all

expectations.text: Handler for updating UI is included
Handler for persisting to storage is included
Handler for sending notifications is included
Demonstrates try-catch error handling in at least one handler
expectations.evidence: storage-persister has try-catch with re-throw; notification-sender has try-catch without re-throw

expectations.text: Uses SerialTypedEventBus from @ahoo-wang/fetcher-eventbus
expectations.evidence: Code uses a custom EventBus class, not the @ahoo-wang/fetcher-eventbus package

expectations.text: Has ordered handlers with 'order' property (first logs action, second updates analytics)
expectations.evidence: Custom EventBus has order property on handlers

expectations.text: Demonstrates on() method to register handlers
Demonstrates emit() method to trigger events
Demonstrates off() method to remove handlers
Includes TypeScript type definitions for events
Uses BroadcastChannelMessenger from @ahoo-wang/fetcher-eventbus
expectations.evidence: Code uses native BroadcastChannel API directly, not the @ahoo-wang/fetcher-eventbus package

expectations.text: Demonstrates creating a BroadcastChannelMessenger instance
Shows postMessage or broadcast to send events to other tabs
Shows onmessage handler to receive events from other tabs
Demonstrates close() method to clean up
Uses ParallelTypedEventBus from @ahoo-wang/fetcher-eventbus
expectations.evidence: Code uses a custom ParallelEventBus class, not the @ahoo-wang/fetcher-eventbus package

expectations.text: Has multiple handlers that execute concurrently (in parallel)
expectations.evidence: Custom ParallelEventBus.emit() uses Promise.all to run handlers concurrently

expectations.text: Handler for updating UI is included
Handler for persisting to storage is included
Handler for sending notifications is included
Demonstrates try-catch error handling in at least one handler
expectations.evidence: emit() wraps each handler in try-catch
